ip_outcome_note: Card name 'Secret Treaty Hercule' names the character explicitly. DBS card database lists Special Trait 'Earthling/World Tournament' and Era 'The Evil Wizard Babidi Saga' — the Majin Buu arc in which Hercule/Mr. Satan's shadow deal with Good Buu is the central B-plot. Vision's panicked comedic male figure with wild hair matches Hercule's well-documented design across Dragon Ball Z and Dragon Ball Super.

trivia_bullets.text: DBS-TB02 World Martial Arts Tournament is Bandai's second themed booster for the Dragon Ball Super Card Game, released September 21, 2018. The set spans multiple tournament eras — each color covers a different in-universe tournament — with 77 unique card designs printed across Normal and Foil variants totaling 125 printings per box run. [dbs-cardgame.com product page; retailer listings via WebSearch]

Hercule (known in Japan as 'Mr. Satan') first won the 24th World Martial Arts Tournament legitimately — defeating Spopovich and Jewel in a bracket the Z-Fighters did not enter. He then leveraged that victory into celebrity status and held the championship title from the 24th through the 31st tournaments, though his 25th and later wins came when Z-Fighters deliberately threw their matches to maintain his cover. [Wikipedia: Mr. Satan; CBR 'The World Protector's Mr. Satan, Explained']
sources: CBR article 'The World Protector's Mr. Satan, Explained'

trivia_bullets.text: TB2-010 is a Red Battle Card (Power 5000, Energy Cost 1) with the Special Trait Earthling/World Tournament. Its game effects are: [Permanent] This card can be comboed from your Battle Area even in Rest Mode; [Activate:Main] Choose this card and 1 'World Tournament' in your Battle Area, switch both to Rest Mode: Draw 1 card. The card's title 'Secret Treaty' maps directly to the canonical Hercule-Goku understanding — Hercule publicly claims credit for Z-Fighter victories in exchange for keeping the world's saviors anonymous. [dbs-cardgame.com card database via WebSearch]

The card's era field is listed as 'The Evil Wizard Babidi Saga' — placing it at the 25th World Martial Arts Tournament in DBZ canon, where Hercule competed alongside the Z-Fighters but where Spopovich and Yamu's energy-drain attack on Gohan interrupted the proceedings entirely. [DBS card game database via WebSearch]
sources: DBS card game database via WebSearch

trivia_bullets.text: Community sentiment on r/DragonBallSuperCardGame: Hercule cards in TB2 are generally treated as support filler for World Tournament themed decks rather than standalone powerhouses — the 1-cost draw-engine role is appreciated for smoothing early game hands, but Hercule's low power ceiling (5000) limits his combat utility. [WebSearch results referencing TB2-010 retailer listings and deck discussion snippets]
sources: WebSearch retailer listings and community snippets for TB2-010

related_cards.name: Secret Treaty Android 18
related_cards.note: TB2-009 — parallel 'Secret Treaty' naming convention in the same set; Android 18's World Tournament agreement to throw her 25th tournament match mirrors Hercule's broader arrangement with the Z-Fighters

related_cards.name: World Tournament Arena
related_cards.note: TB2-068 — the Extra card venue that powers the entire World Tournament trait engine in TB2

related_cards.name: Announcer, Play-By-Play Pro
related_cards.note: TB2-067 — the Announcer Leader Card whose Permanent ability reduces energy costs for all World Tournament cards, making Hercule synergy viable
